privileged_info_summary: This is a synthetic data cleaning task with a CSV file containing 134 customer transactions from 85 unique customers (after name normalization). The task requires consolidating duplicate entries where the same customer appears with different name formatting (e.g., 'John Smith', 'JOHN SMITH', 'J. Smith', 'Smith John'). Actual data characteristics: 134 transactions with 43 customers having duplicates/variations, $20,370.56 total revenue, three date formats (MM/DD/YYYY: 23, DD-MM-YY: 44, YYYY-MM-DD: 66), three currency formats ($X.XX: 87, X.XX USD: 2, numeric: 45). VIP threshold is the 80th percentile of customer spending ($359.94), which identifies 17 of 85 customers (20.0%) as VIP. Required output: cleaned_customer_data.csv with columns CustomerID, CleanedName, TransactionDate, CleanAmount, VIP_Status, PaymentMethod. Pass threshold is 75% (6 of 8 verification criteria). Key contradictions: README claims ~150 transactions (actual: 134), ~45 customers (actual: 85), 8-15 duplicates (actual: 16 exact or 49 with variations), ±1% revenue tolerance (verifier uses 2%).
